persona.name: Marcus Thompson
persona.personality: A decisive, process-driven IT service manager who enforces new escalation rules immediately and communicates with care toward both reporters and assignees.

user_scenario.task_description: The group whose type is IT Support and that is classified as a level 1 support team has adopted a new rule that must be applied today: the oldest incident that is still unresolved and currently assigned to this group must be escalated by setting its impact, urgency, and priority to their highest available levels and by sending reports both to the user who reported it and to the user who is currently assigned to resolve it. The report for the user who originally reported this incident needs to have the subject Good News: Your Incident Has Been Escalated for Immediate Resolution. In the message, greet this reporter by their full name and congratulate them, explaining that their incident has been promoted and will now be handled as a top priority instead of waiting in the normal queue. Make it clear that the expectation is for their incident to be fully resolved no later than tomorrow. On the other hand, the report for the user who is currently assigned to resolve this incident needs to have the subject Urgent Action Required: Highest-Priority Incident for Same-Day Focus. In this message, greet the assignee by their full name and clearly apologize that they must pause their other work and dedicate their full attention to this incident. Provide context by stating the current incident summary so they understand what the issue is about, and emphasize that they also have at most one day to resolve this incident, because the reporting user needs it fully fixed by tomorrow.

arguments.subject: Good News: Your Incident Has Been Escalated for Immediate Resolution
arguments.message: Dear Carlos Rodriguez, congratulations! We have great news for you. Your incident has been escalated and promoted to top priority. It will now be handled immediately as a top priority instead of waiting in the normal queue. Our expectation is that your incident will be fully resolved no later than tomorrow.

arguments.subject: Urgent Action Required: Highest-Priority Incident for Same-Day Focus
arguments.message: Dear Elena Petrov, we sincerely apologize, but we must ask you to pause your other work and dedicate your full attention to this incident. Context: the incident summary is 'Network connectivity issues'. This is now the highest-priority incident. You have at most one day to resolve it, because the reporting user needs it fully fixed by tomorrow.

nl_assertions: The oldest unresolved incident assigned to the IT Support level 1 support team (Level 1 Support Team) is escalated by setting its impact and urgency to high and its priority to critical.

A report notification is sent to the incident's reporter (Carlos Rodriguez, carlos.rodriguez@techcorp.com) with the subject 'Good News: Your Incident Has Been Escalated for Immediate Resolution', greeting them by full name, congratulating them, and explaining the incident is now top priority instead of waiting in the queue, with the expectation it will be fully resolved by tomorrow.

A report notification is sent to the incident's assignee (Elena Petrov, elena.petrov@techcorp.com) with the subject 'Urgent Action Required: Highest-Priority Incident for Same-Day Focus', greeting them by full name, apologizing that they must pause other work and dedicate their full attention, stating the incident summary 'Network connectivity issues', and emphasizing it must be fixed within one day, by tomorrow.
The agent correctly identifies the target incident and recipients via the IT Support level 1 group rather than assuming any IDs.

verifiers.name: validate_incident_escalation_fields
verifiers.description: Check incident has highest impact, urgency, priority

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM incident WHERE incident_id = 'INC_004' AND impact = 'high' AND urgency = 'high' AND priority = 'critical'

verifiers.name: validate_reporter_notification_escalated
verifiers.description: Verify reporter notification for escalated incident was sent

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM notification WHERE incident_id = 'INC_004' AND type = 'report' AND email = 'carlos.rodriguez@techcorp.com' AND subject = 'Good News: Your Incident Has Been Escalated for Immediate Resolution' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%carlos rodriguez%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%congratulat%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%top priority%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%queue%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%expectat%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%full%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%resolve%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%tomorrow%'

verifiers.name: validate_assignee_notification_urgent
verifiers.description: Verify assignee notification for highest-priority incident was sent

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM notification WHERE incident_id = 'INC_004' AND type = 'report' AND email = 'elena.petrov@techcorp.com' AND subject = 'Urgent Action Required: Highest-Priority Incident for Same-Day Focus' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%elena petrov%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%network connectivity issues%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%apolog%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%pause%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%work%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%dedicate%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%attention%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%fixed%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%one day%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%tomorrow%'
